description: The ten common government-process Agent Skills across NYC. Each orchestrates the REST operations and MCP tools of one or more agencies to complete a citizen task.

skills.name: Apply for a permit, license, or benefit
skills.description: Start and track an application to the city — a building permit, business or professional license, benefit (SNAP/cash/Medicaid), grant, school or college admission, or lottery.

skills.name: Report a problem or file a complaint
skills.description: File a 311-style service request or a complaint/tip with the agency that owns it — noise, a pothole, a building condition, a consumer or rights complaint, a tip to an investigator.

skills.name: Request public records
skills.description: Make a records request — a FOIL request, a vital record (birth/death/marriage), a certified document, or a transcript.

skills.name: Schedule or reserve
skills.description: Book an appointment, inspection, hearing slot, facility, or hold — an OATH hearing, a DOB inspection, a park/venue reservation, a library hold.

skills.name: Register or enroll
skills.description: Register or enroll with the city — register to vote or request a ballot, register a business or vendor, enroll in a program.

skills.name: Dispute or appeal
skills.description: Challenge a city determination — appeal a property-tax assessment, dispute a violation or ticket, respond to a summons, file a grievance.

skills.name: Pay a city charge
skills.description: Pay a fine, fee, tax, or bill owed to the city and get a receipt.

skills.name: Look up a property or place
skills.description: Resolve a NYC address, BBL, or BIN and return everything the city knows about that place across agencies — its districts, who represents it, and its records (buildings, housing, environment, complaints).

skills.name: Check application or case status
skills.description: Look up the status of an application, permit, complaint, case, or request across agencies.

skills.name: Search city data and records
skills.description: Search and read the city's open records and datasets — filings, permits, violations, licenses, budgets, contracts, results — the read surface across all 67 agencies.
